learning_outcomes.description: Understand the value of digital technologies for innovation and entrepreneurship.
Develop a critical understanding of contemporary business environments.
Apply research skills to organise and interpret discipline knowledge.
Use information literacy and communication skills to professionally structure and present thinking.
Function as an effective team member by constructively contributing to teamwork.

assessments.due_date:
Week 3 Tue - Week 12 Tue

Teams lead their respective debates according to their chosen topic cluster leveraging knowledge from a guest specialist, individual & team research & the perspectives of other teams.
assessments.task_description:
In teams of up to 4 people, students will select one of 5 topic clusters to debate the innovation & entrepreneurial opportunities of the respective cluster with other teams, in class. Each topic will be introduced by a guest specialist in one week with the team debate occurring the following week. Each participating team will collaborate for their topic to devise & deliver a class exercise for others to dive deeper into that topic. Presentations will be assessed against the debate rubric linked in Blackboard.

Please Note: Each debate will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

This assessment will evaluate students’ Accomplished Scholar skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum.

AI Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Teams upload any slides & activity resources to Blackboard prior to debate delivery
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>40% Create 1 resource & moderate 3 others, across each of the 5 topic clusters </dd>
assessments.due_date:
AI debate moderations 10/03/2026 - 16/03/2026

Quantum technologies debate moderations 24/03/2026 - 30/03/2026

Digital health debate moderations 21/04/2026 - 27/04/2026

IoT debate moderations 5/05/2026 - 11/05/2026

Crop genetics debate moderations 19/05/2026 - 25/05/2026
assessments.task_description:
After each team debate, each student will craft ONE resource in RiPPLE to describe key elements of the debate as they observed and a key learning or application they derived from that discussion. Focus lies in actionable learning to share with the class

Each student will then moderate THREE resources in RiPPLE as created by other students, with a focus on critically analysing the insights of these resources and adding their own observations towards strengthening that resource.

Create 1 topic resource following each cluster debate and moderate 3 other resources to deepen topic understanding. A2 rubric for resource creation & moderation available in Blackboard.

This assessment will evaluate students’ Influential Communicator skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum. 

AI Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Use the RiPPLE platform, linked to Blackboard, to create 1 resource (on the day of debate) and moderate 3 others (the week after the debate) for each of the 5 topic clusters.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
End of Semester Exam Period

6/06/2026 - 20/06/2026

formative checkpoints contribute to development of the final case chosen by each student for the exam to demonstrate critical thinking, creative problem solving and testing inquiry against the researched case.
assessments.task_description:
The case study allows you to explore & articulate the digital innovations utilised by a live venture and to analyse the entrepreneurial impacts in bringing those innovations to market. You develop your target case through the 2 formative reflection activities (week 4 & week 10 checkpoints) prior to sitting the exam.

The exam will evaluate students’ Courageous Thinkers skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum.

AI Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Hand in exam paper along with notes
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: The exam paper with your notes must be handed to the invigilator at the close of the exam session.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
